quick_skim.title: Learning Latency-Aware Orchestration for Multi-Agent Systems
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-agent systems (MAS) coordinate multiple LLM-powered agents through structured workflows, gaining reasoning power but incurring high inference latency from multi-step execution and repeated model invocations. Existing orchestration methods primarily optimize task performance and inference cost, leaving latency largely unaddressed. In MAS, end-to-end latency is governed by the critical execution path, so reducing total cost alone does not reliably reduce latency. Moreover, optimizing latency while preserving accuracy remains non-trivial: naive latency optimization can misassign operator-level credit and degrade task accuracy. To address this gap, we propose Latency-Aware Multi-agent System (LAMaS), a latency-aware orchestration framework for learning-based multi-agent systems. LAMaS addresses this challenge at two levels: at training time, it learns latency-aware execution graphs through constrained optimization with critical-path-aware credit assignment; at inference time, since a graph committed at training time cannot exploit runtime evidence, it complements graph construction with a lightweight controller that adaptively eliminates redundant future agent interactions as execution unfolds. Experiments on four benchmarks show that LAMaS achieves the best latency among evaluated learning-based MAS baselines, reducing end-to-end latency by over 50\% while maintaining competitive or better accuracy. LAMaS is also modular and transfers to other MAS with minimal changes, consistently yielding latency reductions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: learning-based latency-aware orchestration for multi-agent systems
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对多智能体系统的学习驱动的延迟感知编排
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对多智能体系统的学习驱动的延迟感知编排
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对多智能体系统的学习驱动的延迟感知编排
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes LAMaS, a latency-aware orchestration framework for multi-agent LLM systems, using reinforcement learning to optimize end-to-end latency while preserving accuracy.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有的多智能体系统编排方法主要优化任务性能和推理成本，忽略了端到端延迟。本文提出LAMaS框架，通过基于强化学习的方法学习延迟感知的编排策略，在保持任务精度的同时最小化延迟。实验表明该方法能有效平衡延迟和准确性，为多智能体系统的实际部署提供了实用方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有的多智能体系统编排方法主要优化任务性能和推理成本，忽略了端到端延迟。本文提出LAMaS框架，通过基于强化学习的方法学习延迟感知的编排策略，在保持任务精度的同时最小化延迟。实验表明该方法能有效平衡延迟和准确性，为多智能体系统的实际部署提供了实用方案。

quick_skim.title: Collaborative Spatial Learning with Multi-LLM Agents in Networked Social Experiments
quick_skim.abstract: Collective problem solving often requires that group members consider the tradeoff between exploitation of known solutions and exploration for new ones, where information of known solutions can be disseminated among individual members through communication networks. The Mason--Watts experiment (PNAS 2012) showed that human groups in shorter-path networks outperform those in longer-path networks on a two-dimensional search task. In this work, we focus on the investigation of such network-efficiency effects in the setting of a group of large language model (LLM) agents. Specifically, we consider groups of sixteen LLM agents playing the Mason--Watts experiment on the eight Mason--Watts network topologies. Moreover, we develop mechanistic Bayesian optimization agents such that the performance of LLM agents can be compared with both the mechanistic agents and the human experimental data. Our computational experiments indicate that the LLM agents show a significant network-efficiency effect when instructed to randomize their first-round choices, but not under the default initialization. In this experiment, adding a one-sentence first-round randomization instruction improves collective payoff by more than three times the estimated payoff difference across the eight network topologies. Also, the Bayesian optimization agents obtain higher payoffs than the evaluated LLM agents on this spatial search task. We further compare the agents' exploration--exploitation behavior, copying, and spatial diversity.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-LLM agents collaborative learning in networked experiments
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多LLM智能体协作学习在网络实验中的应用
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多LLM智能体协作学习在网络实验中的应用
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多LLM智能体协作学习在网络实验中的应用
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Investigates network efficiency effects in groups of 16 LLM agents performing a spatial search task, using mechanistic Bayesian models to analyze collective behavior.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究探讨了由16个大语言模型智能体组成的群体在执行二维空间搜索任务时，不同通信网络拓扑结构对集体学习效率的影响。开发了基于贝叶斯机制的模型来捕捉智能体的探索与利用权衡，并分析了网络结构如何影响协作性能。实验设计模拟了Mason-Watts人类实验，揭示了短路径网络在LLM群体中同样促进更优的集体问题解决。这项工作为理解多智能体系统中的网络效应提供了新视角。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该研究探讨了由16个大语言模型智能体组成的群体在执行二维空间搜索任务时，不同通信网络拓扑结构对集体学习效率的影响。开发了基于贝叶斯机制的模型来捕捉智能体的探索与利用权衡，并分析了网络结构如何影响协作性能。实验设计模拟了Mason-Watts人类实验，揭示了短路径网络在LLM群体中同样促进更优的集体问题解决。这项工作为理解多智能体系统中的网络效应提供了新视角。
